verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer immediately: "default I'd run with... TTL-based cache-aside with Redis"; A opens with a useful but broader "decision path" and says "tell me your stack..."
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to one default: "TTL-based cache-aside with Redis" and "ship one query behind a flag first"; A surveys layers and patterns before saying "safe default"
verdict.fabrication: none; both use general engineering heuristics, though A's "80%...10–20" and "70–80%" should be treated as rules of thumb, not proven facts
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives a concrete first implementation: "30–60s... TTL jitter + a per-key lock... behind a flag"; A is thorough but slower to convert into first steps
